IND:vllm@1656ad370455->ca179d0f6474.note: 这是引入 commit；下述问题由它引入，后被修复。
IND:vllm@1656ad370455->ca179d0f6474.mechanism: choose_mp_linear_kernel() returns a class (type object), not an instance. The guard `isinstance(kernel_type, MarlinLinearKernel)` tests whether a *class* is an instance of MarlinLinearKernel, which is always False, so the block setting `mp_linear_kernel_config.act_type = input_dtype` (from get_marlin_input_dtype/VLLM_MARLIN_INPUT_DTYPE) never ran. This silently disabled the Marlin W4A8 int8/fp8 activation-quantization fast path, keeping compressed-tensors W4A16 layers on the slower W4A16 activation path even when the user opted in. The fix `kernel_type is MarlinLinearKernel` correctly compares class identity, re-enabling the intended activation-quant path.

manifest_conditions.dtype: W4A16 weights with int8/fp8 activation quant (W4A8) via VLLM_MARLIN_INPUT_DTYPE
manifest_conditions.hardware: SM89/SM120 for fp8 path (per get_marlin_input_dtype); any Marlin-capable GPU for int8

IND:Megatron-LM@642fdd901032->9ff763aa42.note: 这是引入 commit；下述问题由它引入，后被修复。
IND:Megatron-LM@642fdd901032->9ff763aa42.mechanism: In `_CudagraphReplayNode.forward`, each cudagraph replay copies user inputs into the graph's static input buffers via `cudagraph_input.copy_(user_input)`. When the user input tensor already aliases the graph buffer (identical `data_ptr()`), this is a redundant device-to-device self-copy on the hot per-replay path. This commit restores the `if user_input.data_ptr() != cudagraph_input.data_ptr():` guard around the copy so the memcpy is skipped when nothing changed.

IND:Megatron-LM@f456199700bc->947bdbbd2e.note: 这是引入 commit；下述问题由它引入，后被修复。
IND:Megatron-LM@f456199700bc->947bdbbd2e.mechanism: Megatron-FSDP mixed-precision dtype args (main_params/main_grads/grad_comm) are supposed to be passed into DistributedDataParallelConfig, which the FSDP adapter reads to build its MixedPrecisionPolicy. The predecessor #3903 moved these fields into DDPConfig and removed the training.py block that populated them from args.*, but never added them to the kwargs dict used to construct DDPConfig — so the user's --megatron-fsdp-*-dtype flags (notably grad_comm_dtype, which controls gradient gather/scatter comm precision) were silently ignored and fell back to defaults, forcing higher-precision/higher-volume grad communication than requested. This commit adds the three kwargs back to restore the intended (lower-precision) comm path.

manifest_conditions.dtype: custom grad_comm/main_grads dtype (e.g. BF16) set via --megatron-fsdp-*-dtype flags; regression forced fp32/default

IND:vllm@0838b52e2eff->87789c836422.note: 这是引入 commit；下述问题由它引入，后被修复。
IND:vllm@0838b52e2eff->87789c836422.mechanism: Before the `-O` optimization-level infrastructure landed, `--enforce-eager` only disabled torch.compile and CUDA graphs. That infra (#26847 / 0838b52e2) added `OptimizationLevel` and made `enforce_eager` force `optimization_level = O0`. But O0 (OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL_00) disables far more than compile/cudagraphs — it turns off all fusion passes (fuse_norm_quant, fuse_act_quant, fuse_allreduce_rms, fuse_attn_quant, enable_sp, fuse_gemm_comms, fuse_act_padding) and flashinfer autotune (`enable_flashinfer_autotune: False`). So enabling `--enforce-eager` silently also disabled these kernel-level optimizations, degrading throughput/latency even though eager execution alone shouldn't preclude them. The fix decouples this: `--enforce-eager` now only sets `compilation_config.mode = CompilationMode.NONE` and `cudagraph_mode = CUDAGraphMode.NONE`, leaving `optimization_level` at its default (O2) so fusion passes and flashinfer autotune stay on.

manifest_conditions.hardware: fusion benefits notably TP>1 Hopper+ (fuse_allreduce_rms) and quantized/FP8 paths

IND:vllm@d9d21eb8e35f->a9b4f07ba259.note: 这是引入 commit；下述问题由它引入，后被修复。
IND:vllm@d9d21eb8e35f->a9b4f07ba259.mechanism: Late-interaction (ColBERT-style) scoring computes MaxSim (q@d.T, max over doc tokens, sum over query tokens) over per-token embeddings. A prior fast path computed MaxSim on the worker/GPU, avoiding shipping large per-token embedding tensors back to the API server for CPU-side scoring. The preceding refactor commit d9d21eb8e (#28631) rewrote scoring/serving.py from scratch and dropped this GPU path, so scoring fell back to the slow CPU-side _post_process. This commit re-enables it: adds FlashLateInteractionIOProcessor (whose _post_process returns outputs unchanged because scoring is done on the worker) and a flash_late_interaction orchestration that (1) encodes+caches query token embeddings on workers, (2) encodes docs and returns scalar scores from workers, pinning same-query requests to the same engine (crc32(query_key)%num_engines). Enabled by default via enable_flash_late_interaction=True.

manifest_conditions.hardware: GPU (MaxSim moved from server CPU to worker GPU)
manifest_conditions.parallelism: multi-engine: same-query requests pinned to one engine via crc32%num_engines

IND:vllm@a97954b6a8fa->bdd8981dab8d.note: 这是引入 commit；下述问题由它引入，后被修复。
IND:vllm@a97954b6a8fa->bdd8981dab8d.mechanism: In the torch.compile precompile artifact-loading path, the AOTAutograd/functorch config used at compile time (e.g. bundled_autograd_cache, strict_autograd_cache, force_non_lazy_backward_lowering, and the full save_config_portable() snapshot) affects the AOTAutograd cache key and compilation behavior. The prior commit a97954b6a (#35810) stored aot_autograd_config and re-applied it in the mega-artifact and lazy optimized_call paths, but MISSED the eager finalize_loading() path. This commit wraps the VllmBackend re-compilation in finalize_loading with `functorch_ctx` (torch._functorch.config.patch(saved_aot_autograd_config)) so the stored config is applied consistently, and removes the hardcoded config_patches (bundled_autograd_cache/strict_autograd_cache/force_non_lazy_backward_lowering) in piecewise_backend.py that were band-aiding cache-key consistency. Without it, config mismatch during finalize causes AOTAutograd cache-key divergence and unnecessary recompilation on artifact load.

manifest_conditions.feature: torch.compile precompile / AOT artifact caching (VLLM_USE_MEGA_AOT_ARTIFACT / cached artifact load path)
